message:
test(harness): conformance ext4 symlink targets vs debugfs

New harness ext4_symlink_kernel_reference plants 11 symlinks via
`debugfs -w symlink` and verifies that Ext4ImageReader::read_symlink
returns byte-for-byte identical targets to what e2fsprogs reports for
the same inode. The corpus straddles the 60-byte fast/extent boundary
explicitly: one-byte target, max-1, exact max (60 B), max+1 (the first
extent-mapped case), 200 B and 1024 B fills, plus a heterogeneous
ASCII payload that catches byte-equality regressions monotonic fills
would hide.

Cross-check methodology:
  * fast symlinks: parse `Fast link dest: "..."` from `debugfs stat`
    output (e2fsprogs emits the target verbatim on one line);
  * extent-mapped symlinks: dump the data block via `debugfs dump`
    and compare against the corpus.

Also asserts the structural invariants the FFS code relies on:
is_fast_symlink() ⇔ size ≤ 60 B, fast symlinks lack the EXTENTS flag,
extent-mapped ones carry it. Self-skips on missing ext4 userland.

Existing kernel_reference covers superblock, dirs, file content,
inode metadata, xattr, extent mapping, dir/inode/extent-block
checksums and dx_hash, but never exercised the symlink read path
end-to-end against e2fsprogs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
